title: Second Conditional — hypothetical present
subtitle: Grammar of the day
body: Use the second conditional for imaginary or unlikely situations in the present or future. The key: past tense in the if-clause, would in the result.

examples.word: If I had a million dollars, I'd travel the world.
If I were you, I'd apologize.
examples.translation: consejo — nota: 'were' para todas las personas
